ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED FOR PURPOSES OF ENTERING TENTATIVE RULING) HEARING SET FOR AUG-31-2023 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 503

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-31-2023 IN 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS DECEMBER 18, 2023, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS DECEMBER 29, 2023. LOCAL RULE 20 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS DECEMBER 14, 2023. THE COURT WILL ALLOW A MAXIMUM OF FOUR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS PER DAY TO BE CALENDARED IN THIS ACTION. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS DECEMBER 1, 2023. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS DECEMBER 15, 2023. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SCHEDULED FOR OCTOBER 26, 2023, AT 1:30 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 14, 2023, AT 9:00 A.M. IN 603. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS GRANTED EXCUSE BASED ON GOOD CAUSE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc.
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Asbestos Default Prove-Up Hearing / Plaintiff Thomas McCollum's Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc.

Dept. 304, Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: J. Alameda. Reported: For The Record. Bailiff: Not present.

Kimberly Chu, Esq., of Brayton Purcell, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Thomas McCollum.

Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement at Default Prove-up hearing on file.

No appearance by defaulted defendants KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. and K-M INDURSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.

Default of Defendants has been duly entered.

The Court states that it has reviewed the default packet, the CIV-100 Judicial Council form, and the Declaration of James P. Nevin. The Court admits all exhibits to the Declaration of James P. Nevin in Support of the Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc., filed on August 15, 2025.

After evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendants KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. and K-M INDURSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. in the amount of:

As to KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.,
$70,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $2,070,500.00

As to K-M INDURSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.,
$70,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $2,070,500.00

Judgment signed in open court.

(The total judgment of $2,070,500.00 is as to each defaulted defendant.)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Appearances via Court Call: Fred Gatt of Leader Berkon Colao & Silverstein LLP (213.234.1750) for IMO Industries, Inc.; Jessica Robles of Foley & Mansfield PLLP (213.283.2100) for Riley Power, Inc.; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

The trial date is December 18, 2023, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 29, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 14, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.

The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.

Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.

If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone.

Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.

Fact discovery cut-off date is December 1, 2023.

Expert discovery cut-off date is December 15, 2023.

All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 28, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

No later than September 14, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than September 14, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.

A further case management conference is scheduled for October 26, 2023, at 1:30 p.m. There is no need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar.

Plaintiffs are to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than November 3, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 17, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by December 8, 2023, at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, December 14, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause.

Order signed. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/SKF)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED FOR PURPOSES OF ENTERING TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling

MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. As of the date of the granting of Plaintiff's motion, the First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K.
